get.description:
Liveness probe — does NOT check the database. The container's
liveness signal should stay green during a transient Postgres
outage so Azure doesn't restart the pod into a thundering-herd
reconnect storm. Returns the deploy SHA when one is configured.

Readiness probe — confirms Postgres is reachable. Synthetic
monitors that page on database loss should target this endpoint
(not /healthz). Azure Container Apps reads /readyz to decide whether
to route traffic to the revision.

parameters.name: adopter_status

schema.description: Filter adopters by status. Repeatable: ?adopter_status=new&adopter_status=matched. Ignored when party_kind=facilitator.
schema.title: Adopter Status

parameters.description: Filter adopters by status. Repeatable: ?adopter_status=new&adopter_status=matched. Ignored when party_kind=facilitator.

parameters.name: facilitator_status

schema.description: Filter facilitators by status. Repeatable. Ignored when party_kind=adopter.
schema.title: Facilitator Status

parameters.description: Filter facilitators by status. Repeatable. Ignored when party_kind=adopter.

get.description:
Aggregate status counts for the pipeline filter chips.

Returns ``{counts: {status: n, ...}, total: N}`` for the requested
party kind. NULL statuses are aggregated under ``__unset__`` so the
UI can render a "no status" bucket without losing rows.

Why a separate endpoint instead of computing client-side from
``/v1/contacts``: that endpoint paginates (default limit=50), so a
client can't sum statuses by walking the list. Sums need a server-
side aggregate, full stop.

parameters.name: party_kind

schema.description: Which party kind to count by status. Required — the two kinds have different status enums and the response shape depends on it.
schema.title: Party Kind

parameters.description: Which party kind to count by status. Required — the two kinds have different status enums and the response shape depends on it.

post.description:
#40: HTTP surface for ``match_or_route()``. Required for the staff UI to
re-run matching after a send-back or manual contact create. Idempotent in
spirit — repeated calls during the same run produce the same outcome
(capacity check is read-inside-transaction).

get.description:
List active facilitating orgs. Ordering is alphabetical by ``name``
for stable display.

post.description:
Grant a B2C subject access to one facilitator org. 409 if the grant
already exists (the table has a composite primary key).

delete.description:
Revoke a facilitator-org membership. Idempotent: returns 204 whether
or not the row existed (so re-runs of an onboarding script are safe).

Soft delete: flip status to ``archived`` so audit history stays
intact. Hard delete is reserved for failed-test cleanup.

MagicLinkErrorDetail.title: MagicLinkErrorDetail
MagicLinkErrorDetail.description:
Error envelope for the magic-link endpoints. Mirrors the
``HTTPException(detail={"code": ..., "message": ...})`` shape so the
generated TS client gets a typed error body to switch on.

MatchCandidate.title: MatchCandidate
MatchCandidate.description:
A ranked alternative for a single adopter_interest. Includes the
persisted MatchAttempt score so the UI can show "why this match" without a
second round-trip.

MatchSummary.title: MatchSummary
MatchSummary.description: One open Match row in the queue, with embedded alternatives.

IntakeBearerKey.description: Intake API key, sent as ``Authorization: Bearer <key>``. The key must match one of the comma-separated entries in the server's ``INTAKE_API_KEYS`` setting.
